## ThetaDataDx 9.1.0

Single-queue SSOT rewrite for FPSS streaming (closes #513). The
documented "single-dispatcher SSOT" claim previously composed two
queues — the LMAX Disruptor ring plus a
`crossbeam_channel::bounded(8192)` inside `StreamingDispatcher` —
with a per-tick `FpssEvent::clone` between them. Resolved through an
external multi-model audit.

### Changed

- `ThetaDataDx::start_streaming` invokes the user callback directly
  from the LMAX Disruptor consumer thread, each invocation wrapped in
  `std::panic::catch_unwind`. Exactly one queue between the TLS
  reader and the user callback.
- `dropped_event_count()` now reports `Producer::try_publish`
  failures (ring-buffer overflow) instead of crossbeam `Full`
  rejections. Same public signature.
- Data-event publishes use `Producer::try_publish` so a slow user
  callback can never block the TLS reader. Handshake-time control
  frames keep the original blocking `publish` so the
  `Connected -> Ping -> LoginSuccess` wire ordering is preserved.

### Added

- `ThetaDataDx::panic_count()` and `FpssClient::panic_count()` —
  snapshot the count of user-callback panics caught by the Disruptor
  consumer's `catch_unwind` boundary. Each panic is also surfaced via
  `tracing::error!` with target `thetadatadx::fpss::io_loop`.
- `expert-mode` Cargo feature on `thetadatadx`, off by default.
  Reserves `start_streaming_inline` for a future TLS-reader-direct
  dispatch path; documented as forbidden for `stop_streaming`,
  `reconnect_streaming`, blocking, or panicking callbacks. The FFI
  crate enables this feature unconditionally so
  `tdx_*_set_inline_callback` keeps its C ABI.
- `crates/thetadatadx/tests/streaming_soak.rs` — four soak tests
  (slow callback, panicking callback, callback-triggered stop, burst
  overload) that exercise the consumer-thread wiring without a live
  FPSS connection.
- Python SDK `sdks/python/README.md`: "Streaming buffering" section
  documenting the `collections.deque` (Pattern A, default) and
  `queue.Queue` (Pattern B, cross-thread blocking) consumer patterns.

### Removed

- `crates/thetadatadx/src/fpss/dispatcher.rs` and its public exports
  (`StreamingDispatcher`, `DispatcherProducer`). Panic isolation,
  drop counting, and consumer-thread invariants now live on the
  Disruptor consumer.
- `crossbeam-channel` runtime dependency on `thetadatadx`.

### Performance

- Per-event cost on the `start_streaming` path drops by removing the
  `event.clone()` + `crossbeam` `try_send` + drain-thread wakeup hop
  that previously sat between the Disruptor consumer and the user
  callback.
- Bench coverage at
  `crates/thetadatadx/benches/streaming_channels.rs` covers four
  variants of the new pipeline (Disruptor consumer with
  `catch_unwind`, Disruptor consumer without `catch_unwind`, direct
  callback, cross-thread Disruptor publish).

### Verification

Reviewed by an external multi-model audit; soak tests cover the four
contracts the rewrite preserves (no reader stall, no consumer panic
kill, no self-join deadlock, exact drop counting under burst).
